children.content: Australians, including Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Opposition Leader Angus Taylor, gather to commemorate Anzac Day at Dawn Services this morning. Follow live.

items.title: Sniper who killed top bikie in public execution revealed after gag on identity lifted

children.content: The former soldier who publicly assassinated ex-Rebels bikie boss Nick Martin at Perth Motorplex in 2020 can be publicly identified for the first time, after he successfully overturned a court order suppressing his identity.

image.altText: A smiling man wearing a cap and white shirt on the right poses in a selfie with a quokka on the left.

items.title: Perth woman warns about pedicure safety after severe infection

children.content: Kyla Willcox says she almost lost her toe from a potentially fatal infection after having a pedicure at a Perth nail salon.

image.altText: Foot with big toe covered in white bandage

items.title: AI traffic fines set to net $440m in driver fines across Perth and WA

children.content: The cameras have handed out around 184,000 infringements since they started issuing fines in October last year, according to the WA government.

items.title: Woodside tells inquiry no projects 'would survive' with additional tax

children.content: Oil and gas giant Woodside denies its business model is "weak" while arguing that paying a proposed flat tax on gas exports would kill the company's projects.

items.title: Extra rangers at Karijini campsites after three dingo attacks within a week

children.content: A camper describes a "brazen" dingo attack on a four-year-old girl in northern Western Australia as officers continue to search for three aggressive dingoes.

articles.title: Three children attacked by dingoes in national park

children.content: Three children have been attacked by dingoes in Karijini National Park since Saturday, prompting a warning from authorities.

items.title: Dozen more possible human burial sites found at Rottnest Island church

children.content: Sports clubs and competitions are reporting lower participation, as players withdraw from training and competition due to high fuel and travel costs.

image.altText: Lois Garner sits down next to an indoor basketball court.

items.title: From the WA bush to the jungle of Vietnam

children.content: Yamatji man Graham Taylor was just 19 when he shipped off to Vietnam. More than 50 years on, the brotherhood forged in the jungle of Vietnam remains solid.

items.title: 'We're not ready to leave': Inside the messy dynamic of a no-grounds eviction

children.content: Evicting a tenant without cause offers landlords a simple way out, but the issue can be much more complex than it appears. One such example of that complexity is Eleanor Kahlo's case of her no-grounds eviction.

items.title: Fight over legal costs from Gina Rinehart judgement begins

children.content: A legal wrangle is underway over what costs mining businesswoman Gina Rinehart is liable for following last week's judgment in her epic court battle with the family of her father's former business partner.

items.title: What happens after you buy a house?

children.content: You've heard it all before: it's harder to buy a house.
But, if you do manage to get your foot on the property ladder, things are also more difficult afterwards.
The housing crisis has turned into an intergenerational wealth crisis, set to make paying your mortgage in retirement the norm.
So how did we get there? And how do we fix it?
